candidates.title: How we think about agentic web infrastructure
candidates.summary: Anthropic's first written stance on the runtime layer for agents — names Cloudflare Durable Objects, Workflows, and Temporal as the categories worth watching.

candidates.title: Pricing storage on Durable Objects: what we learned in 18 months
candidates.summary: Cloudflare retrospective on Durable Objects storage pricing, with concrete numbers on per-instance memory + I/O patterns from real workloads.

candidates.title: Inngest v3: durable workflows for the agent era
candidates.summary: Inngest pivots its workflow product toward agent loops — checkpoint resume, parallel tool dispatch, retry semantics tuned for LLM tool calls.

candidates.title: Show HN: Running Claude tool loops on Temporal (1200pts)
candidates.summary: Top-of-frontpage HN post on using Temporal as the durable executor for Claude tool loops; comments split between 'overkill' and 'finally'.

candidates.title: Actor-Based Coordination for Long-Running Agent Workloads
candidates.summary: Survey-leaning paper comparing actor-model frameworks (Erlang/Elixir, Akka, Durable Objects) for hosting agent runtimes; benchmarks across 4 workloads.

candidates.title: The Workers + Durable Objects pattern for agent applications
candidates.summary: Simon's walkthrough of running a Claude-driven agent on Workers + Durable Objects with code samples. Strong endorsement of the pattern.

candidates.title: The Agent Runtime Wars: who wins, who pivots, who dies
candidates.summary: Opinionated piece naming Cloudflare, Modal, and Temporal as the runtime-layer winners; pre-mortems Vercel functions for agent workloads.
